evidence.page_title: OAuth 2.0 Authentication | Zoho CRM API | V8
evidence.content:
The Zoho CRM API uses the **OAuth 2.0** protocol for authentication. **OAuth 2.0** is an industry-standard protocol specification that enables third-party applications (clients) to gain delegated access to protected resources in Zoho via an API.

- [Why should we use OAuth 2.0?](https://www.zoho.com/crm/developer/docs/api/v8/oauth-overview.html#why)
- [How OAuth 2.0 works?](https://www.zoho.com/crm/developer/docs/api/v8/oauth-overview.html#how)
- [Key Terminologies in OAuth 2.0](https://www.zoho.com/crm/developer/docs/api/v8/oauth-overview.html#terms)
- [Tokens](https://www.zoho.com/crm/developer/docs/api/v8/oauth-overview.html#tokens)

- Clients are not required to support password authentication or store user credentials.
- Clients gain delegated access, i.e., access only to resources authenticated by the user.
- Users can revoke the client's delegated access anytime.
- **OAuth 2.0** access tokens expire after a set time. If the client faces a security breach, user data will be compromised only until the access token is valid.

![OAuth 2.0 Workflow](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/crm/oauth-2-workflow.jpg)

The following are some terms you need to know before you start using the Zoho CRM APIs.
evidence.section_path: OAuth 2.0 for V8 APIs - An Overview > Key Terminologies in OAuth 2.0

evidence.page_title: OAuth 2.0 Authentication | Zoho CRM API | V8
evidence.content:
The Zoho CRM resources, such as Leads, Contacts, Deals, etc.

The Zoho CRM server that hosts protected resources.

Any end-user of your account, who can grant access to the protected resources.

An application that sends requests to the resource server to access the protected resources on behalf of the end-user.

The consumer key generated from the connected application.

The consumer secret generated from the connected application.

Authorization server provides the necessary credentials (such as Access and Refresh tokens) to the client. In this case, it will be the Zoho CRM authorization server.

The authorization server creates a temporary token and sends it to the client via the browser. The client will send this code to the authorization server to obtain access and refresh tokens.

A token that is sent to the resource server to access the protected resources of the user. The Access token provides secure and temporary access to the protected resources and is used by the applications to make requests to the connected app. Each access token will be valid only for an hour and can be used only for the set of operations that are described in the scope.

A token that can be used to obtain new access tokens. This token has an unlimited lifetime until it is revoked by the end-user.

**Important**

User Access Token must be kept confidential since it provides access to protected resources. Do NOT expose your Access Token anywhere in public forums, public repositories or on your website's client-side code like HTML or JavaScript. Exposing it to the public may lead to data theft, loss or corruption.

At Zoho, we take data privacy and security seriously at every level of our business, from product development to vendor standards. We also comply with all applicable data protection laws and regulations.

[LEARN MORE](https://www.zoho.com/privacy-commitment.html)

Data integrity is important to us—that's why we handle all user data using the latest [international security compliance](https://www.zoho.com/security.html) standards, with [100% data encryption](https://www.zoho.com/encryption.html) in transit, using the latest version of TLS.

We have a strict [privacy policy](https://www.zoho.com/privacy.html) to protect personal information, and we never put our users at risk by selling or disclosing their information to unauthorized third parties. We comply with GDPR and CCPA worldwide, going above and beyond what’s mandated by law.

Zoho owns and operates its own [data centers](https://www.zoho.com/know-your-datacenter.html)—we do not run on a public cloud. Our unified data model and unified cloud storage allows for shared services like AI, search, analytics, and messaging to scale alongside our users.
